name: Texas Skillet Corn Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat bacon drippings in a large well-greased black iron skillet.
2. In a large mixing bowl, mix cornmeal, flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda, and sugar.
3. Add buttermilk and stir rapidly until combined.
4. Add eggs and mix until just incorporated.
5. Add hot drippings and stir rapidly until the batter is smooth.
6. Pour batter into the skillet (see note) and cover with foil.
7. Reduce heat to low.
8. Cook on top of the stove for about 5 to 6 minutes, or until the bread is lightly browned and almost cooked through—enough that you can turn it easily with a wide spatula or pancake turner.
9. Turn and brown on the other side for about 2 minutes.
10. Cut into wedges to serve. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Note: If you have an electric range, heat two burners—one hot and the other on low. When you pour the batter into the hot skillet, immediately move to the low-heat burner to finish cooking.
